title: Authenticated users can persist arbitrary web push endpoints that the web-push reminder cron later requests with service-role context

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: RLS lets authenticated users manage their own web_push_subscriptions rows, including endpoint/p256dh/auth/timezone.

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: Normal client writes PushSubscription endpoint into web_push_subscriptions; direct authenticated PostgREST writes can target the same table/columns.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Table defines endpoint as unconstrained text with no scheme/host/IP allowlist or check constraint.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Scheduled function passes only the x-cron-secret boundary, then service-role reads all stored subscriptions.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Stored endpoint is copied into pushSub.endpoint and passed to webPush.sendNotification.

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated user can insert or update their own web_push_subscriptions row through Supabase Data API under owner_all RLS; endpoint is stored as text.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: Scheduled service-role web-push sender performs outbound webPush.sendNotification to the stored endpoint without repository evidence of destination validation, allowlisting, scheme restriction, DNS/IP checks, or redirect/metadata blocking.
impact: Potential SSRF/callback abuse from the Supabase Edge Function network context, including probing or requesting internal/cloud metadata/LAN endpoints if the web-push library/runtime permits those destinations. At minimum the scheduled service can be induced to make attacker-chosen outbound requests.
closest_apparent_control: Cron secret protects triggering the batch, but does not protect the stored endpoint sink because the attacker controls data consumed by the scheduled job. RLS enforces row ownership but not endpoint destination safety. Browser PushManager normally produces legitimate push endpoints, but direct authenticated Data API writes are permitted by the table grants/policy.
why_plausible: The source/control/sink path is visible in repository code: owner_all permits user-managed subscription rows; endpoint has no DB constraint; the cron function reads all rows with service-role and passes sub.endpoint directly to webPush.sendNotification.
candidate_local_validation_evidence: Static trace completed. Dynamic SSRF reproduction was not run in this discovery-only worker. Proof gap: exact web-push library/runtime behavior for non-standard endpoints, redirects, private IPs, and metadata hosts should be tested in validation.

candidate_local_attack_path_facts.entrypoint: Supabase Data API write to public.web_push_subscriptions plus scheduled web-push-reminders cron execution
candidate_local_attack_path_facts.trust_boundary: stored user-controlled endpoint crosses into service-role scheduled outbound request
preconditions: web_push_reminders cron secret exists and scheduled job runs
attacker can create a due reminder or otherwise have dueEntries for their subscription
web-push library accepts attacker-selected endpoint form
counterevidence: The normal app path obtains endpoint from browser PushManager
web-push protocol may reject malformed or non-HTTPS endpoints
candidate_local_attack_path_facts.counterevidence_disposition: These narrow exploitability but do not close direct authenticated writes to the subscription table or prove destination allowlisting before sendNotification.

title: Coach push cron reuses user-controlled web push endpoints for outbound sends without destination validation

affected_locations.label: source
affected_locations.detail: Authenticated users can manage their own web_push_subscriptions rows.

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: endpoint is text and there is no DB destination constraint.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Scheduled coach-push function authenticates cron secret, checks feature flag, then service-role reads all stored subscription endpoints.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Stored sub.endpoint is passed directly to webPush.sendNotification for each subscription.

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated user-controlled web_push_subscriptions endpoint row, reachable through the same owner_all RLS/table grants as the standard PWA subscription flow.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The coach-push scheduled function makes outbound web-push requests to stored endpoints without repository evidence of destination allowlist, private-address blocking, scheme checks, or endpoint provenance validation.
impact: Potential SSRF/callback abuse from a second scheduled service-role function. Because coach-push also includes personalized/progress context and deletes stale subscriptions on 404/410, destination abuse can be triggered independently of regular reminder sends when the feature flag and signal window permit it.
closest_apparent_control: x-cron-secret and feature flag gate function execution; they do not constrain the endpoint data already persisted by authenticated users. Normal browser-generated endpoints are not equivalent to a server-side enforcement control for direct Data API writes.
why_plausible: The repository shows the source table is user-writeable, the endpoint column is unconstrained, and coach-push passes sub.endpoint to webPush.sendNotification without a repository-local allowlist or validator. This is independently reachable from web-push-reminders because coach-push has its own cron secret, feature flag, signal selection, and send sink.
candidate_local_validation_evidence: Static trace completed. Dynamic reproduction was not run in this discovery-only worker. Proof gaps: coach_push feature flag runtime value, signal-window preconditions, and web-push library handling of attacker-chosen endpoints need validation.

candidate_local_attack_path_facts.entrypoint: Supabase Data API write to public.web_push_subscriptions plus scheduled coach-push cron execution
candidate_local_attack_path_facts.trust_boundary: stored user endpoint to service-role outbound request
preconditions: coach_push flag is enabled or can become enabled by admin rollout
attacker has data/signals that make a coach push eligible
counterevidence: coach_push is seeded disabled in migration 20260612120000_coach_push_sends.sql
cron secret blocks direct unauthenticated execution
candidate_local_attack_path_facts.counterevidence_disposition: The feature flag is a deployment precondition, not a code-level destination control; the same persisted endpoint sink remains when the feature is enabled.
